title: 7 Herbal Creams For Morning Sickness
intro_desc: If you're experiencing morning sickness during pregnancy, herbal creams can be a welcome relief. These creams often contain soothing herbs that are known to ease nausea and vomiting. But have you ever wondered how herbal teas can relieve morning sickness too? It's because these herbs have properties that help calm the stomach and reduce queasiness. For example, ginger (Zingiber officinale) is a natural anti-inflammatory that can help reduce nausea and vomiting. Fennel (Foeniculum vulgare) is another herb that can help ease digestive issues and reduce morning sickness symptoms. Peppermint (Mentha x piperita) is also known to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea. When you apply these herbs in a cream form, they can be absorbed directly into the skin, providing quick relief from morning sickness symptoms. This means that you can get on with your day without feeling queasy or vomit-inducing. The benefits of using herbal creams for morning sickness are numerous - you'll be able to eat and drink without feeling sick, and you'll also be able to manage your stress levels, which can be a major contributor to morning sickness. By using these creams, you can take control of your morning sickness symptoms and live a more comfortable and stress-free life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiemetic effects, helping to alleviate nausea and vomiting. Gingerols, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the release of serotonin, a hormone that can cause stomach upset. Shogaols, on the other hand, have been found to have a direct effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the stomach muscles. By consuming Zingiber officinale creams, pregnant women may experience relief from morning sickness due to the soothing and calming effects of its active compounds.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for morning sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale powder in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove the double boiler from heat and stir in the Zingiber officinale powder until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool, and store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains compounds like estragole, limonene, and anethole, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These active constituents have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate the stomach cramps and nausea associated with morning sickness. Foeniculum vulgare creams also contain vitamins and minerals like vitamin B6, which plays a crucial role in regulating nausea and vomiting. The anti-inflammatory properties of fennel's compounds can also help soothe digestive issues, reducing the discomfort caused by morning sickness. The antispasmodic effects of fennel's compounds can help relax the smooth muscles in the stomach, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with morning sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for morning sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare (fennel seeds) and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Heat 1 cup of carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the heated oil and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Store the Foeniculum vulgare cream in a glass jar and apply 1-2 teaspoons as needed to help alleviate morning sickness symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains menthol and menthone, which are active constituents that help alleviate morning sickness. Menthol's cooling and numbing properties can provide relief from nausea and vomiting, making it easier for women to manage their symptoms. Menthone, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe digestive issues and reduce stomach discomfort. The menthol in Mentha x piperita creams can also help to calm the stomach and reduce spasms that contribute to nausea and vomiting. By applying Mentha x piperita creams to the wrists or temples, women can experience quick relief from morning sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for morning sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil, and a small bottle for the cream.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Stir in the peppermint essential oil.
Pour the mixture into the small bottle and let it cool completely until it's solid.
Apply a small amount of the cream to the skin as needed to help with morning sickness symptoms. Store the cream in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a bioactive constituent that has anti-inflammatory properties. Glycyrrhizin helps to reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the action of stomach acid and soothing the digestive tract. The licorice root also contains flavonoids and polyphenols, which have antioxidant properties that can help to alleviate morning sickness symptoms. Glycyrrhizin's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of morning sickness. By applying Glycyrrhiza glabra creams to the skin, the active ingredients are absorbed into the bloodstream, providing relief from morning sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for morning sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Melt the beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once the ingredients are melted, remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, vitamin E oil, and lavender oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify before use. Store the cream in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavoglycosides and bilobalide, two main bioactive constituents that help alleviate morning sickness. Flavoglycosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce nausea and vomiting caused by hormonal changes during pregnancy. Bilobalide, on the other hand, has a vasodilating effect, which can improve blood flow to the brain and reduce dizziness and lightheadedness often associated with morning sickness. The anti-inflammatory properties of flavoglycosides can also help reduce stomach cramps and discomfort often experienced during this time. By using ginkgo biloba creams, pregnant women may find relief from the symptoms of morning sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for morning sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Add the shea butter and Ginkgo biloba extract to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely. Apply a small amount to your skin as needed to help relieve morning sickness symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two potent active constituents that have a soothing effect on the body. These compounds work by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate the nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness. The calming properties of linalool and linalyl acetate also help to regulate digestion and reduce stomach upset, providing relief from the discomfort of morning sickness. Additionally, lavender's analgesic and antispasmodic properties can help to ease cramps and spasms in the stomach, further reducing symptoms of morning sickness. By using Lavandula angustifolia creams, women experiencing morning sickness may find natural relief from their symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for morning sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before adding lavender and peppermint essential oils. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Lavandula angustifolia cream is ready to use. Apply 1-2 tablespoons as needed to help alleviate morning sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contain essential oils like rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and camphor. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness. Rosmarinus officinalis also contains antioxidants like linalool and borneol, which can help to stabilize blood sugar levels and reduce dizziness. The anti-spasmodic properties of rosmarinic acid can help to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramps and discomfort. By applying Rosmarinus officinalis creams to the skin, the beneficial compounds can be absorbed quickly, providing fast relief from morning sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for morning sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the melted mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Let the mixture cool and solidify. Once solid, scoop it into a container and store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with morning sickness. Peppermint tea, in particular, has been known to ease nausea and stomach discomfort. Ginger tea is also a popular choice as it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the stomach. Some women find these teas helpful in reducing morning sickness symptoms.
faq_safe: Some herbal teas, like ginger and peppermint, can help with morning sickness. However, drinking them every day can have side effects like stomach upset and dizziness. It's also possible for herbal teas to interact with other medications or worsen underlying health conditions. Use them in moderation and pay attention to how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile are often used to help with morning sickness. Some women notice a difference after one or two cups a day, while others may take a few days to feel the effects. Everyone's body is different, so it's hard to say exactly how long it will take.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for morning sickness can be most helpful in the morning, as soon as you wake up. A warm cup of peppermint or ginger tea can settle your stomach and help ease nausea. It's a good habit to start your day with a soothing cup of herbal tea, which may give you a much-needed relief from morning sickness symptoms.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for morning sickness, especially if you're taking a lot of different herbs. For example, peppermint tea might make your stomach problems worse if you're already taking medication to reduce nausea. Always be aware of what you're putting in your tea and how it might affect your body.
faq_pregnant: Herbal teas can be a helpful way to ease morning sickness during pregnancy. However, some teas may not be safe. Peppermint and ginger teas are often recommended as they can help settle your stomach. But teas like pennyroyal and sage should be avoided, as they can cause problems for you and your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for morning sickness can be a good option while breastfeeding. However, some herbs can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. Peppermint, ginger, and chamomile are generally considered safe, but it's best to check the tea's ingredients and instructions. Be cautious with herbs like raspberry leaf and pennyroyal, as they might have unknown effects.
title_2: Morning Sickness Remedies for New Moms: 7 Herbal Creams to Try
